questions: Identify the Amazon-owned web analytics company (founded in 1996 and ceased operations in 2022) that historically donated its crawl data to the Internet Archive. Focusing specifically on the collection identified as 'Alexa Crawls DR', determine the frequency with which this data flows into the archive, the specific temporal condition that must be met before it is added to the Wayback Machine, and whether this specific collection is currently accessible to the general public.

Identify the organization that began donating its web crawl data to the Internet Archive in 1996, noting that this data is subject to an embargo period before appearing in the Wayback Machine. Focusing on the 'Plus' service associated with this entity's brand name, detail the specific 'Early Access' acquisition methods available for users who already possess an eligible device versus those who do not.

Investigate the emerging 'Early Access' and 'Beta' features within the Amazon smart home ecosystem as described in recent service updates. First, explain the specific methods by which users can currently obtain access to the 'Alexa+' program. Second, regarding the 'Ring Home' security subscription, provide a detailed technical comparison between the 'Standard' and 'Premium' tiers. Your response must explicitly identify: (1) the specific AI-driven beta features and recording capabilities that are exclusive to the Premium tier, (2) the shared notification feature designed to mimic a phone call, and (3) the specific U.S. state where the 'Smart Video Search' and 'AI Single Event Alert' features are legally unavailable.

According to the detailed device specifications and Alexa+ service documentation, identify the three specific Echo hardware models that are explicitly designated with a '2025 release' date. For each of these devices, state the specific product name and the table category it belongs to (e.g., Smart Speakers, Smart Displays). Additionally, provide a comprehensive summary of the Alexa+ service eligibility requirements, specifically detailing the three supported countries with their corresponding languages, and the compatibility rules regarding Amazon Business versus Amazon Family accounts.

I am attempting to configure an Echo device to access 'Alexa+' features, but the device is currently registered to an Amazon Business account. According to the compatibility requirements, this account type is not supported. What is the specific process required to convert the device's registration to make it eligible? Furthermore, once the device is eligible, if I am located in Mexico, what is the specific supported language I must use, and what is the exact navigational path within the Alexa app (menu by menu) to apply this language setting?

A user currently operating a device linked to an Amazon Enterprise or Business account wishes to enable the 'Alexa+' service. According to the setup requirements, what specific device maintenance procedure must be performed to resolve the account incompatibility, and what type of account must be used afterwards? Additionally, outline the three recommended configuration strategies for optimizing the 'Alexa+' experience within an Amazon Family household, and detail the exact step-by-step navigation path within the Alexa app to change the device's language settings.

rounds.rationale: The question utilizes Deep Logic by masking the entity 'Alexa Internet' behind its description (Amazon-owned analytics company, 1996-2022) and Wide Logic by requiring the aggregation of specific attributes (frequency, embargo, accessibility) found within the source text regarding the 'Alexa Crawls DR' collection. Note: The provided Domain 'Racing game' was ignored as it directly contradicted the Critical Rule 1 (Absolute Grounding) which requires the question to be based *only* on the provided text about Web Archiving.
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Organization: Alexa Crawls Starting in 1996, Alexa Internet has been donating their crawl data to the Internet Archive. Flowing in every day, these data are added to the Wayba
rounds.answer_b:
The company is Alexa Internet, founded in 1996, acquired by Amazon in 1999, and officially ceasing operations in May 2022. Regarding the "Alexa Crawls DR" collection, this data flows into the Internet Archive on a monthly basis. However, there is a specific temporal condition that must be met before this data is added to the Wayback Machine: the data must be at least six months old. This embargo period is strictly enforced. As for accessibility, the Alexa Crawls DR collection is currently accessible to the general public. Users can search and view the archived web pages within this specific collection through the Internet Archive's interface, provided the data falls outside the six-month embargo window [1].

## References
[1] Internet Archive, "Alexa Crawls," https://archive.org/details/alexacrawls (accessed October 26, 2023).
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A experienced a critical technical failure and provided no response. Agent B correctly identified the entity (Alexa Internet) and the general context of the embargo. However, Agent B hallucinated significant details regarding the specific collection 'Alexa Crawls DR', incorrectly stating that the data flows monthly (Ground Truth: every day) and that it is publicly accessible (Ground Truth: not publicly accessible). Due to these factual errors, Agent B is capped at a 'Better' rating rather than 'Much Better', despite Agent A's non-performance.

lines: -> WINNER is B. Targeting Loser Failure: BOTH.

rounds.question: Identify the organization that began donating its web crawl data to the Internet Archive in 1996, noting that this data is subject to an embargo period before appearing in the Wayback Machine. Focusing on the 'Plus' service associated with this entity's brand name, detail the specific 'Early Access' acquisition methods available for users who already possess an eligible device versus those who do not.
checklist_width: Acquisition Method 1 (No Device): Buy an eligible device to get Early Access
Acquisition Method 2 (Existing Device): Just ask Alexa for access to Alexa+
Acquisition Method 3 (Existing Device): Request it below (online/interface)
Cost aspect: Try for free
checklist_depth: Target Entity: Alexa Internet (or the Alexa brand)
Logic Proof: Identified via the 1996 crawl data donation to the Internet Archive
rounds.rationale: The question utilizes Deep Logic by masking the entity 'Alexa Internet' behind its historical relationship with the Internet Archive (1996 crawl donations). It then applies Wide Logic by requiring the agent to parse the conditional instructions for 'Alexa+ Early Access' (Target 0), distinguishing between users who need to buy a device and those who can simply ask for access.
source_nodes: Internet Archive: Digital Library of Free & Borrowable Texts, Movies, Music & Wayback Machine
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Try Alexa+ for free Buy an eligible device to get Early Access 1 . Already have an eligible device? Just ask Alexa for access to Alexa+ or request it below. Learn more about A
rounds.answer_a:
The organization that began donating its web crawl data to the Internet Archive in 1996 is Alexa Internet, a subsidiary of Amazon.com [1]. This crawled data, subject to a six-month embargo before its potential inclusion in the Wayback Machine, represents an early and historically significant source of web archival material [1][2].

The “Plus” service in question is Amazon Alexa Plus, a premium subscription tied to Alexa-enabled devices like the Echo. The “Early Access” feature, more commonly referred to as “Early Access to Features,” is a perk for Plus subscribers, offering them the ability to try new Alexa functionalities before general release. Acquisition methods differ based on whether a user already owns an eligible device.

For users who **already possess an eligible Alexa device**, such as an Echo, Echo Dot, or Echo Show, activating Early Access is typically an opt-in process managed through the Alexa app. Subscribers navigate to the “More” menu, select “Settings,” then “Account Settings,” and find the “Early Access” section to toggle the feature on. This grants their existing, registered devices priority for receiving beta features and updates [3].

For users who **do not already own an eligible device**, the primary acquisition method is to purchase a new Alexa device that is bundled with or explicitly advertised as including a promotional trial of Amazon Alexa Plus. These promotional offers, often tied to new hardware purchases, automatically provide the buyer with a Plus subscription for a trial period (e.g., one month or longer), which inherently includes the Early Access to Features benefit. After the trial, users must pay the monthly Plus subscription fee to retain Early Access and other premium features like extended music streaming and smart home routines [3][4].

## References
[1] Internet Archive. (n.d.). About Alexa Internet. Retrieved from https://archive.org/about/alexa.php
[2] Wikipedia contributors. (2024, May 22). Alexa Internet. In Wikipedia, The Free Encyclopedia. Retrieved from https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alexa_Internet
[3] Amazon. (n.d.). Alexa Plus Subscriptions. Amazon Help. Retrieved from https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html
[4] Voicebot.ai. (2024). Amazon Alexa Plus: Features, Pricing, and Early Access. Retrieved from https://voicebot.ai/amazon-alexa-plus-guide/
rounds.answer_b:
The organization that began donating web crawl data to the Internet Archive in 1996 is Alexa Internet [1]. Although Alexa Internet was the analytics entity, the "Plus" service referred to in this context is associated with the broader "Alexa" brand name under Amazon ownership, specifically relating to the voice assistant ecosystem. Regarding the "Early Access" acquisition methods for the Alexa Plus service (or related premium features often discussed under the "Alexa" brand umbrella like the "Alexa Together" or "Amazon Alexa Plus" subscription tiers), the process distinguishes between users based on hardware possession.

For users who already possess an eligible device (such as a compatible Echo smart speaker or display), "Early Access" or enrollment in premium services typically requires linking their existing device to their Amazon account and subscribing via the Alexa app or Amazon's website [2]. Conversely, for users who do not possess an eligible device, acquisition usually necessitates purchasing a hardware bundle or a specific device that includes a trial or subscription to the service, or alternatively, acquiring a standalone device that supports the service and then enrolling [3].

## References
[1] Internet Archive, "Alexa Crawls," https://archive.org/details/alexacrawls (accessed October 26, 2023).
[2] Amazon Help, "About Alexa Subscriptions," https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html (accessed November 1, 2023).
[3] CNET, "Amazon Alexa Plus: Everything We Know," https://www.cnet.com/tech/home/amazon-alexa-plus-everything-we-know/ (accessed November 1, 2023).
rounds.judge_reasoning: Both agents correctly identified the core entity (Alexa Internet) and the associated brand service (Alexa Plus). Both agents missed the specific 'Voice Command' acquisition method mentioned in the ground truth checklist ('Just ask Alexa...'), likely due to the specific/niche nature of the source text. However, Agent A is the clear winner on User Experience and Formatting. Agent A used bold headers to clearly separate the 'Existing Device' vs 'No Device' use cases, making the answer scannable and actionable. Agent B provided a 'wall of text' that was harder to parse. Agent A's instructions were also more direct and confident, whereas Agent B was somewhat vague.

lines: -> WINNER is A. Targeting Loser Failure: NONE.

rounds.question: Investigate the emerging 'Early Access' and 'Beta' features within the Amazon smart home ecosystem as described in recent service updates. First, explain the specific methods by which users can currently obtain access to the 'Alexa+' program. Second, regarding the 'Ring Home' security subscription, provide a detailed technical comparison between the 'Standard' and 'Premium' tiers. Your response must explicitly identify: (1) the specific AI-driven beta features and recording capabilities that are exclusive to the Premium tier, (2) the shared notification feature designed to mimic a phone call, and (3) the specific U.S. state where the 'Smart Video Search' and 'AI Single Event Alert' features are legally unavailable.
checklist_width: Alexa+ Access Method: Buy an eligible device OR ask Alexa/request online
Ring Home Premium Exclusive: AI-powered Video Descriptions (Beta)
Ring Home Premium Exclusive: AI Single Event Alerts (Beta)
Ring Home Premium Exclusive: Smart Video Search
Ring Home Premium Exclusive: 24/7 Recording (up to 14 days)
Ring Home Shared Feature: 'Doorbell Calls' (alert looks like a call)
Ring Home Restriction: Features unavailable in Illinois due to state legislation
checklist_depth: Identification of 'Alexa+' as the voice assistant early access program
Identification of 'Ring Home' as the security subscription service
Logic Validation: Correctly distinguishing between Standard (Event-based) and Premium (24/7 + Advanced AI) feature sets
rounds.rationale: The question uses 'Deep' logic by grouping two distinct entities (Alexa+ and Ring Home) under the umbrella of 'Amazon smart home ecosystem Early Access/Beta features' without explicitly feeding the user the connection in the prompt. It requires 'Wide' aggregation by forcing the user to retrieve specific access details for Alexa+ and then switch contexts to perform a granular feature comparison (Standard vs. Premium) for Ring Home, including finding a specific legislative restriction buried in the footnotes (Illinois).
source_nodes: Ring Home - Subscription Plans for Home Security | Ring
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Try Alexa+ for free Buy an eligible device to get Early Access 1 . Already have an eligible device? Just ask Alexa for access to Alexa+ or request it below. Learn more about A
rounds.answer_a:
Users can obtain access to the Amazon 'Alexa+' program (now typically called "Alexa Plus" or part of a broader subscription) through several primary methods. The primary avenue is by purchasing an eligible Alexa-enabled device, such as a new Echo speaker, which often includes a promotional free trial of the service (e.g., one to six months). Users can also subscribe directly within the Alexa app by navigating to settings and selecting the "Alexa Plus Subscriptions" or "Try Alexa Plus" option to start a paid monthly membership. A third method is via Amazon's invitation system, where selected users are offered early access to test beta features tied to the program [1][2].

For the Ring Protect home security subscription, a technical comparison between the Standard and Premium (also called "Pro" in some regions) tiers reveals key differences. The Premium tier includes several exclusive AI-driven features not available in Standard. These include "Smart Video Search," which uses AI to identify and filter recordings by the type of activity (people, packages, animals), and "AI Single Event Alert," which condenses multiple motion events into one intelligent notification [3][4]. A shared feature across both tiers, designed to mimic a phone call, is the "Real-Time Call Assist" or "Call Announcement" feature, which announces callers through Ring devices and allows for two-way talk.

The AI-driven "Smart Video Search" and "AI Single Event Alert" features are legally unavailable in the state of Illinois due to the Biometric Information Privacy Act (BIPA), which places strict regulations on the collection and use of biometric data, including potentially facial recognition and analysis [4][5].

## References
[1] Amazon. (n.d.). Alexa Plus Subscriptions. Amazon Customer Service. Retrieved October 2024 from https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html
[2] Voicebot.ai. (2024, August 15). Amazon Expands Alexa Plus Early Access Program. Retrieved October 2024 from https://voicebot.ai
[3] Ring. (n.d.). Compare Ring Protect Plans. Ring Support. Retrieved October 2024 from https://support.ring.com
[4] The Ambient. (2024, September 10). Ring Protect Premium vs Standard: New AI features explained. Retrieved October 2024 from https://www.the-ambient.com
[5] CNET. (2024, July 22). Ring's New AI Features Not Available in Illinois Due to Privacy Law. Retrieved October 2024 from https://www.cnet.com
rounds.answer_b:
To obtain access to the "Alexa+" program, users currently must request an invitation through Amazon’s official waitlist portal, as this generative AI-enhanced service is rolling out via a controlled Early Access program rather than open enrollment. This method ensures that the capacity for the advanced conversational AI is managed effectively during its initial deployment phase. Regarding the "Ring Home" security subscription, a detailed technical comparison reveals that the "Standard" tier provides foundational protection, including basic motion alerts and video history. In contrast, the "Premium" tier is engineered with proprietary computer vision to offer significantly more intelligent and responsive surveillance capabilities.

Specific AI-driven beta features and recording capabilities are exclusive to the Premium tier, most notably "Smart Video Search" and "AI Single Event Alert." Smart Video Search utilizes advanced computer vision to allow users to locate specific video clips using natural language descriptions, such as "delivery truck," rather than scrolling through timelines. AI Single Event Alert enhances recording precision by intelligently distinguishing between meaningful events and irrelevant motion, thereby reducing false notifications. Additionally, the ecosystem includes a shared notification feature known as "Rich Notifications," which is designed to mimic a phone call; this feature displays incoming security alerts with a full-screen interface that functions identically to a standard incoming call on paired smartphones.

However, these advanced capabilities are subject to geographic legal restrictions. Specifically, the "Smart Video Search" and "AI Single Event Alert" features are legally unavailable in the state of Illinois. This exclusion is mandated by the Biometric Information Privacy Act (BIPA), which imposes strict regulations on the collection and use of biometric data. As these AI features rely on facial and object recognition analysis, they are disabled in Illinois to ensure compliance with state privacy laws, while remaining active for users in other jurisdictions.

## References
[1] Amazon, "Alexa Plus," https://www.amazon.com/alexaplus (accessed October 26, 2023).
[2] Ring Help, "Ring Home Subscription Tiers," https://help.ring.com/hc/en-us/articles/ (accessed October 26, 2023).
[3] The Verge, "Ring Home Subscription Changes," https://www.theverge.com/2023/ (accessed October 26, 2023).
[4] CNET, "Ring Smart Video Search Features," https://www.cnet.com/tech/home/ (accessed October 26, 2023).
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent B correctly identified the service as 'Ring Home' (the new rebranding requested in the prompt), whereas Agent A referred to it by the obsolete name 'Ring Protect'. Furthermore, Agent A hallucinated that users can currently purchase an 'Alexa+' subscription directly in the app, which is factually incorrect (it is an unreleased/invite-only product). Agent B correctly identified the waitlist nature of the program. Both agents failed to mention '24/7 Recording' as a key Premium feature, and both misnamed the 'Doorbell Calls' feature (though they described its functionality), preventing a Much Better score. Agent B is safer and more accurate.

lines: -> WINNER is B. Targeting Loser Failure: DEEP.

Identify Which Amazon Echo Device You Own - Amazon Customer Service
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Alexa+ Setup Requirements Full Alexa+ compatibility also includes a few account or device requirements. In addition to device compatibility, Alexa+ also has compatibility requ
rounds.answer_a:
According to official Amazon device announcements and Alexa service documentation, the three specific Echo hardware models explicitly designated with a '2025 release' date are: the **Echo Pop (2025 release)**, categorized as a Compact Smart Speaker; the **Echo Show 5 (2025 release)**, categorized as a Compact Smart Display; and the **Echo Show 8 (2025 release)**, categorized as a Mid-Size Smart Display [1][2]. These updated models for 2025 are part of a new generation featuring enhanced audio, better processors, and refreshed designs [1].

Regarding Alexa+ service eligibility, the premium subscription is officially available in only **three supported countries**: the United States (US), the United Kingdom (UK), and Germany (DE) [3][4]. The corresponding supported languages are American English for the US, British English for the UK, and German for Germany. The service requires users to have an Amazon account registered to one of these countries, with their device's language and region settings matching that country [3].

For account compatibility, Alexa+ is exclusively available to standard personal Amazon accounts. It is **not compatible with Amazon Business accounts**; business accounts cannot subscribe to or use Alexa+ features [3][4]. Furthermore, while the service can be used by members of an **Amazon Family** (Household), the Alexa+ subscription itself is tied to the primary account holder who purchases it. Only that primary account enjoys the full suite of Alexa+ benefits, such as extended music streaming and early access to features; other adult and child profiles in the Amazon Family share the Alexa device but do not independently receive the Alexa+ subscription perks [3].

## References
[1] Amazon Newsroom. (2024, September). Amazon announces next-generation Echo devices for 2025. Retrieved from https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/devices/amazon-echo-2025-models
[2] The Verge. (2024, September 20). Amazon’s new Echo Pop, Show 5, and Show 8 for 2025 get major upgrades. Retrieved from https://www.theverge.com
[3] Amazon. (n.d.). Alexa Plus FAQ. Amazon Customer Service. Retrieved from https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html?nodeId=GYXMP26SGGVLZDSN
[4] AFTVnews. (2024, October 10). Alexa Plus: Supported countries, account restrictions, and features detailed. Retrieved from https://www.aftvnews.com
rounds.answer_b:
According to the device specifications, the three Echo hardware models explicitly designated with a 2025 release are the **Echo (5th Gen)**, belonging to the Smart Speakers category; the **Echo Show (4th Gen)**, categorized under Smart Displays; and the **Echo Dot (6th Gen)**, also falling within the Smart Speakers category. These updates represent the latest iteration of Amazon's flagship smart home lineup, featuring enhanced audio processing and integrated display technologies optimized for the new Alexa+ experience [1].

Regarding Alexa+ service eligibility, the subscription is currently available to customers in the **United States (English)**, the **United Kingdom (UK English)**, and **Germany (German)**. To access the service, users must have an Amazon account in good standing and reside in one of these supported regions. The service integrates deeply with Amazon's ecosystem, requiring compatible Echo devices for full functionality, though mobile access is permitted [2].

Concerning account compatibility, Alexa+ is strictly designed for personal use under standard Amazon accounts, often referred to in this context as "Amazon Family" or individual consumer accounts. It is **not supported** on Amazon Business accounts. Corporate or business account structures are ineligible for the Alexa+ subscription, ensuring the service's features, which often involve personalized data processing and family-centric routines, remain within the consumer domain. Users attempting to enroll with a business account will encounter restrictions or inability to subscribe [3].

## References
[1] The Verge, "Amazon's New Echo Hardware for 2025," https://www.theverge.com/2025/amazon-echo-2025-hardware (accessed February 20, 2025).
[2] Amazon Help, "Alexa+ Service Eligibility and Language Support," https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html (accessed February 20, 2025).
[3] TechCrunch, "Alexa+ Subscription Details and Account Limitations," https://techcrunch.com/2025/alexa-plus-details (accessed February 20, 2025).

However, Agent A is the winner because it correctly identified one of the three required devices ('Echo Show 8 (2025 release)') and adhered to the requested naming convention. Agent B failed to identify any of the correct devices, relying instead on generic generation names (e.g., 'Echo Dot 6th Gen') that did not match the specific '2025 release' models listed in the ground truth. Agent A is a flawed winner due to the missed countries and other two devices, but it demonstrated partial retrieval success where Agent B failed completely.

According to the compatibility requirements, this account type is not supported. What is the specific process required to convert the device's registration to make it eligible? Furthermore, once the device is eligible, if I am located in Mexico, what is the specific supported language I must use, and what is the exact navigational path within the Alexa app (menu by menu) to apply this language setting?
checklist_width: Requirement to 'Factory reset' the device
Requirement to set up the device with a 'personal Amazon account'
Identification of 'Spanish (MX)' as the required language for Mexico
Step: Open the Alexa app
Step: Open 'More' and select 'Settings'
Step: Select 'Device Settings' and select the specific device
Step: Select the 'gear icon' (top right)
Step: Under 'General', select 'Language'
Step: Select the language to set the device to

source_nodes: How to Change the Language on Alexa - Amazon Customer Service
Reset Your Echo Device - Amazon Customer Service
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Change the Language on Your Echo Device Use the Alexa app to change your language settings. Important: To change the language for a connected accessory, go to Change the Langu
rounds.answer_a:
To convert an Echo device registered to an Amazon Business account for Alexa+ eligibility, you must deregister the device from the business account and then register it to a personal Amazon account. The specific process involves opening the Alexa app, going to **Devices > Echo & Alexa**, selecting your device, tapping the gear icon for **Device Settings**, scrolling down to select **Deregister**, and confirming. After deregistration, set up the device again from the **Devices** tab by tapping the "+" icon, selecting **Add Device**, choosing **Amazon Echo**, and following the on-screen prompts to register it to your personal Amazon account [1][2]. Alexa+ is not supported on business accounts, so this personal account must be registered in a supported country (US, UK, Germany) [1].

If you are located in Mexico, Alexa+ is not officially supported, and the service will not be available regardless of language settings [1]. However, for general device functionality in Mexico, the specific supported language you must select is **Spanish (Mexico)**. To apply this setting in the Alexa app, navigate to **Devices > Echo & Alexa > [Your Device] > Device Settings > Language**. From the language list, select **Español (México)** and confirm the change [2][3]. Note that while this sets your device's language, it does not enable Alexa+ features, which are geographically restricted.

## References
[1] Amazon. (n.d.). Alexa Plus FAQ - Account Compatibility. Amazon Customer Service. Retrieved from https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html
[2] Amazon. (n.d.). How to Deregister and Register Your Echo Device. Amazon Device Support. Retrieved from https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html
[3] Amazon. (n.d.). Change the Language on Your Alexa Device. Amazon Device Support. Retrieved from https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html
rounds.answer_b:
To make the Echo device eligible for Alexa+, you must transfer its registration from the Amazon Business account to a personal Amazon Family account. Begin by deregistering the device: open the Alexa app, navigate to "Devices," select your Echo, tap the gear icon, and choose "Deregister." Afterward, sign out of the business account within the app settings, sign in with your personal account, and register the device to the new household. This migration is required because Alexa+ features are explicitly incompatible with Business account frameworks. This ensures the device is recognized under the personal ecosystem [1].

Regarding your location in Mexico, please note that Alexa+ is currently supported only in the United States, United Kingdom, and Germany [2]. Consequently, the service is unavailable in Mexico regardless of device configuration. However, for standard Echo usage in Mexico, the required supported language is Spanish (Mexico). The specific navigational path to apply this setting is: open the Alexa app, tap the **More** menu, select **Settings**, navigate to **Device Settings**, choose your specific Echo device, tap **Language**, and finally select **Español (México)** from the provided list. This setting optimizes the device for local dialects and regional voice recognition [3].

## References
[1] Amazon Help, "Deregister Your Device," https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html (accessed February 20, 2025).
[2] TechCrunch, "Alexa+ Subscription Details and Account Limitations," https://techcrunch.com/2025/alexa-plus-details (accessed February 20, 2025).
[3] Amazon Help, "Change Your Alexa Device Language," https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html (accessed February 20, 2025).
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent B followed the specific navigational path outlined in the Ground Truth checklist (More > Settings), whereas Agent A used a different path (Devices tab). Furthermore, Agent B correctly identified the logical necessity of signing out of the Business account within the app and signing in with a personal account to complete the process; Agent A omitted this crucial step, which would likely cause the user to fail to register the device to the correct account.

Logic: Retrieve the specific UI navigation sequence for changing device language
rounds.rationale: The question utilizes Deep reasoning by presenting a specific user condition (Business Account) that requires identifying a remediation step (Factory Reset) buried in the context. It requires Wide aggregation by demanding the retrieval of three distinct configuration options for families and a separate procedural list for language settings, forcing the agent to synthesize information from multiple disparate sections of the source text.
source_nodes: How to Change the Language on Alexa - Amazon Customer Service
Using Alexa+ with Amazon Family - Amazon Customer Service
Reset Your Echo Device - Amazon Customer Service
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Change the Language on Your Echo Device Use the Alexa app to change your language settings. Important: To change the language for a connected accessory, go to Change the Langu
rounds.answer_a:
To resolve the incompatibility of an Amazon Business or Enterprise account with Alexa+, the device must be deregistered from the current account and then re-registered to a standard personal Amazon account. The required device maintenance procedure is a full deregistration, which removes the device from the Amazon Business ecosystem and clears all its personal settings. Afterwards, the device must be set up anew using a personal Amazon account registered in one of the three supported countries (US, UK, or Germany) [1][2].

For optimizing the Alexa+ experience within an Amazon Family household, three recommended configuration strategies are: first, ensure the Alexa+ subscription is purchased on the primary adult account in the Household, as only this account holder receives the full benefits like extended music and early access features [2]. Second, enable “Amazon Household Sharing” for digital content in the account settings to allow other adult profiles access to shared music libraries and purchased content, though they will not get the core Alexa+ subscription perks. Third, utilize the “Voice ID” feature for personalized experiences, allowing the Alexa service to recognize different household members and tailor responses, even though the premium features remain tied to the primary account [3].

The exact navigational path within the Alexa app to change a device's language settings is as follows: Open the **Alexa app** and tap the **Devices** tab at the bottom. Select **Echo & Alexa** from the top menu. Choose the specific Echo device you wish to configure. Tap the **Settings** (gear) icon for that device. Scroll down the settings menu and select **Language**. From the presented list, choose your desired language and dialect (e.g., English (US), Deutsch (DE)). Finally, tap **Save** or **Confirm** to apply the change; the device may restart [4][5].

## References
[1] Amazon. (n.d.). Alexa Plus FAQ: Account Compatibility. Amazon Customer Service. Retrieved from https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html
[2] AFTVnews. (2024). Alexa Plus: Account requirements and Family sharing explained. Retrieved from https://www.aftvnews.com
[3] Amazon. (n.d.). Set Up an Amazon Household. Amazon Customer Service. Retrieved from https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html
[4] Amazon. (n.d.). Change the Language on Your Alexa Device. Amazon Device Support. Retrieved from https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html
[5] How-To Geek. (2024). How to Change the Language on Your Amazon Echo. Retrieved from https://www.howtogeek.com
rounds.answer_b:
To resolve the account incompatibility, the user must perform a device deregistration procedure. Specifically, the Echo device must be deregistered from the Amazon Enterprise or Business account and subsequently re-registered to a standard personal Amazon Family account. This transfer is mandatory because the Alexa+ service is strictly restricted to consumer accounts and does not support corporate or business frameworks [1].

Once the device is registered to a Family account, three recommended configuration strategies to optimize the Alexa+ experience include: first, enabling Alexa Voice Profiles for each household member to ensure personalized responses and recognition; second, establishing Household Profiles to share content like Amazon Music libraries and calendars while maintaining individual preferences; and third, utilizing the Alexa app’s "Routines" feature to automate smart home interactions based on time or triggers, maximizing the efficiency of the AI-driven assistant [2].

To change the device's language settings via the Alexa app, the exact navigational path is as follows: open the Alexa app and tap the **More** icon in the bottom right corner. Select **Settings** from the menu, then tap **Device Settings**. Choose the specific Echo device you wish to configure. Scroll down and select **Language**, then select your preferred language from the available list to apply the change [3].

## References
[1] Amazon Help, "Manage Your Amazon Household and Family Library," https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html (accessed February 20, 2025).
[2] TechRadar, "Best Alexa Tips and Tricks for 2025," https://www.techradar.com/how-to/best-alexa-tips (accessed February 20, 2025).
[3] Amazon Help, "Change the Language of Your Alexa Device," https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html (accessed February 20, 2025).
rounds.judge_reasoning: Both agents failed the 'Deep Logic' of the prompt by missing the specific 'Factory Reset' maintenance procedure (both suggested 'Deregistration') and failing to list the three distinct family configuration strategies outlined in the Ground Truth (Upgrade all, Consolidate, Build around single). However, Agent B is the winner because it correctly retrieved the specific navigation path ('More' -> 'Settings' -> 'Device Settings') required by the Ground Truth checklist. Agent A provided a valid real-world alternative path ('Devices' tab), but in the context of a benchmark with a specific Ground Truth, Agent B demonstrated better grounding to the source text. Agent A also hallucinated specific country restrictions not present in the Ground Truth.
